jokes.text: [Narrator] Well, that part wasn't true. But they would've.
jokes.setup: Michael claims they already have blueprints drawn up
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately contradicts him with dry honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of AD's self-aware narration style. Economical and immediately undercuts character pretense.

jokes.text: I need you to hire him. [phone cuts to dial tone] Hi. I'm sor- Look, it isn't my idea, it's his.
jokes.setup: Lindsay urgently needs Michael to hire Tobias
jokes.punchline: Phone cuts out mid-plea, creating awkward restart
jokes.explanation: Good awkward timing comedy with phone technology as obstacle.

jokes.text: Tobias singing in falsetto as Mrs. Featherbottom
jokes.setup: Narrator explains Tobias is pretending to be British housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Visual of grown man singing in high voice while cross-dressing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Tobias's oblivious commitment to absurd schemes. Strong visual comedy.

jokes.text: No. She's the only one who can hand wash my delicates. Yeah, the real Tobias wouldn't do that.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Lindsay tell Tobias she knows he's Mrs. Featherbottom
jokes.punchline: Lindsay admits she needs the domestic help too much to reveal the truth
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Lindsay's selfishness while acknowledging the absurdity.

jokes.text: Booyah!
jokes.setup: Michael says Tobias would need to submit a résumé
jokes.punchline: Tobias immediately produces one with enthusiastic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - overprepared and overenthusiastic. 'Booyah!' becomes running gag.

jokes.text: Wow. Gobias Industries. [Hard 'G'] Gobias. Right. I remember, yeah. As in 'Go buy us a cup of-'
jokes.setup: Tobias reveals his company name on résumé
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun name that sounds like 'go buy us' when pronounced correctly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Tobias's oblivious cleverness. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I have rabies, Michael. You know that. Today was day four of my shots.
jokes.setup: Michael tells Starla to focus on keeping her job
jokes.punchline: Starla casually mentions having rabies as if it's minor
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: Ted. Did Ted make an appointment? No, I just work down the hall and- [Tobias] No. Well, then, Ted can get the hell out of this office! You get the hell out!
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Ted has appointment, Tobias jumps in
jokes.punchline: Tobias aggressively kicks out confused coworker
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tobias's overzealous assistant behavior.

jokes.text: Although, if I may, let me take off my assistant skirt and put on my Barbra Streisand in The Prince of Tides ass-masking therapist pantsuit.
jokes.setup: Tobias wants to offer psychological insight
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate metaphor mixing cross-dressing and therapy imagery
jokes.explanation: Peak Tobias - unnecessarily verbose, unintentionally sexual, pop culture reference. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Ancient Chinese Secret, huh? [Both] Ancient Chinese Secret, huh?
jokes.setup: Lindsay mentions store name
jokes.punchline: Both recognize reference to old commercial jingle
jokes.explanation: Nostalgic reference humor. Somewhat cheap but delivered well.

jokes.text: Yeah, I need a tea to give my dingle less tingle. Me quick, want slow. Wait, that's Indian. Tea for dong.
jokes.setup: Gob needs remedy for sexual dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemisms and racist stereotyping attempts
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - crude and culturally insensitive while trying to be clever.

jokes.text: Sweet blade. It's the Sword of Destiny. It's full of ancient magic. Oh, yeah? You into magic?
jokes.setup: Shop owner describes magical sword to stranger
jokes.punchline: Stranger happens to be professional magician
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic setup for the sword subplot.

jokes.text: Still, where'd the lighter fluid come from?
jokes.setup: Gob examines 'magical' sword
jokes.punchline: Notices obvious evidence of stage magic trickery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's professional eye despite being gullible.

jokes.text: Looks like you're looking for dragons, in the future.
jokes.setup: Gob appears with medieval sword
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation about the ridiculous appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Michael deadpan response to family absurdity.

jokes.text: Bleeding? Nope. Yeah, that's blood.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Gob is bleeding
jokes.punchline: Gob denies then immediately confirms obvious bleeding
jokes.explanation: Classic understated escalation. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Michael, if I make this comeback... I'll buy you 100 George Michaels you can teach to drive!
jokes.setup: Gob tries to convince Michael to help with magic show
jokes.punchline: Absurd promise to buy multiple copies of his nephew
jokes.explanation: Peak Gob absurdity - treats people as commodities while making grand promises.

jokes.text: You're losing blood, aren't you? Probably. My socks are wet. Sorry. You'll be sorry! Wait, that doesn't work after his line.
jokes.setup: Gob admits to losing blood through his socks
jokes.punchline: Gob realizes his comeback line doesn't work and acknowledges it
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where character acknowledges failed dramatic exit.

jokes.text: [Narrator] Actually, Maeby had received a company car from the movie studio where she was secretly posing as an executive.
jokes.setup: George Michael says you need license to get around
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals Maeby doesn't need one due to her secret job
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast showing family members living parallel secret lives.

jokes.text: What's the deal with the turban? Huh? [Chuckles] Pretty smart, huh? And-And it's warm too.
jokes.setup: Michael finds father hiding in attic
jokes.punchline: George Sr. wearing turban as disguise and proud of practicality
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag with George Sr.'s misguided pride in obvious disguise.

jokes.text: When do you start being my mole at my company? Actually, Michael turned me down for the job. What? Something to do with their housing order being cut back.
jokes.setup: George Sr. expects Tobias to be his inside man
jokes.punchline: Tobias obliviously reveals the exact bad news George Sr. didn't know
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - delivers devastating news while missing the point entirely.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's a horrible sign- Ow! Damn it!
jokes.setup: George Sr. reacting to bad business news
jokes.punchline: Hits head on low attic beam while processing information
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with attic setting providing obstacle.

jokes.text: We're gonna cover the hydraulic flaps and the riser system after I tell you the basics about how to avoid a hop-on.
jokes.setup: Michael teaching driving basics
jokes.punchline: Treating stair car like normal vehicle with technical driving terminology
jokes.explanation: 'Hop-on' becomes iconic term. Great absurdist driving instruction.

jokes.text: Let's go over the route to the hospital. Ow!
jokes.setup: Michael in pain from stress
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions from driving lesson to medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - lesson becomes emergency in one line.

jokes.text: My baby is sick, but Mother is here to nurse you. Well, now I'm a little sicker.
jokes.setup: Tobias offers motherly care in disguise
jokes.punchline: Michael's revulsion at the inappropriate comfort attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with Tobias's inappropriate maternal behavior.

jokes.text: I'm peeing. Am I peeing?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to maintain control while medicated
jokes.punchline: Complete loss of bodily control while lecturing about control
jokes.explanation: Perfect thematic irony - loses control while obsessing about control.

jokes.text: It may have taken the tingle out of your genitals.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains medication side effects
jokes.punchline: Clinical description echoing Gob's earlier 'dingle tingle' problem
jokes.explanation: Nice callback connecting brother's problems through medical terminology.

jokes.text: You're lucky. He's the best. Well, only in the county. But I'm certainly good enough to take out that appendix of yours.
jokes.setup: Tobias praises Dr. Stein as the best
jokes.punchline: Doctor modestly downgrades his own reputation while maintaining confidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalating medical incompetence foreshadowing.

jokes.text: Hopefully not too slow, because it's already dangerously slow as it is. [Dr. Stein Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Doctor putting Michael on heart-slowing machine
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions patient's heart is already dangerously slow
jokes.explanation: Dark medical comedy with doctor's inappropriate casual attitude toward danger.

jokes.text: Hook! Forgot about that thing, Elephant Man. Gonna have to figure out something to do so that people can look at you without wanting to kill themselves.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder meeting Buster with hook hand
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of Buster's disturbing appearance
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny but cruel assessment of Buster's disability played for shock value.

jokes.text: which many claimed suffered due to its pop-up ads.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder's website selling DVDs
jokes.punchline: Mundane web design criticism of magic performance
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of magical wonder with annoying internet marketing

jokes.text: Booyah!
jokes.setup: George Sr. needs to show employees someone is in control
jokes.punchline: Uses Tobias's enthusiastic catchphrase from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how catchphrases spread through the family.

jokes.text: Tony Wonder appearing from bread loaf and screaming
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder introduces his bread illusion
jokes.punchline: Emerges screaming from baked goods in elaborate magic trick
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual comedy with Tony Wonder's absurd magic persona.

jokes.text: I folded it up into some squares, and I put it in a pouch under my shirt... and then I pull it out, and I make it look like it came out of my skin.
jokes.setup: Buster asking how Tony Wonder made warm bread
jokes.punchline: Tony explains his 'magic' in completely mundane terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of magic - making the mundane sound complex while being completely obvious

jokes.text: I folded it up into some squares, and I put it in a pouch under my shirt and then I pull it out, and I make it look like it came out of my skin.
jokes.setup: Gob asks how Tony Wonder did bread trick
jokes.punchline: Tony Wonder reveals embarrassingly simple method
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of magic mystique through mundane explanation.

jokes.text: I started out as a little 'W.' Somehow I became a big one. I don't even know how it happened.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder giving career advice
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself as letter 'W' in completely earnest manner
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist character moment treating stage name as literal identity.

jokes.text: Enjoy the Hanukkah cookie, man.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder leaving after bread demonstration
jokes.punchline: Calls bread a 'Hanukkah cookie' while leaving dramatically
jokes.explanation: Absurd renaming of bread as holiday cookie - fits Tony's character

jokes.text: My name is Dr. Tobias Fünke and I'll be filling in for Michael, who is not sick. In fact, just today I saw a sliver of his buttocks and they're as fresh and firm as a Georgia peach, so not to worry.
jokes.setup: Tobias addressing worried employees
jokes.punchline: Reassures them through inappropriate sexual observation about Michael
jokes.explanation: Peak Tobias - completely inappropriate sexual comment delivered as professional reassurance.

jokes.text: Now, put your foot on the brake. There's a light in a half mile. [Brakes Grinding]
jokes.setup: Lindsay teaching George Michael to drive
jokes.punchline: Instructs him to brake way too early, causing grinding sounds
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Lindsay's complete incompetence as instructor.

jokes.text: In this magic trick, I'm going to put a sword... through my assistant's tummy.
jokes.setup: Buster performing magic trick
jokes.punchline: Casually announces he's going to stab Gob in the stomach
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Buster's innocent delivery of violent act

jokes.text: It's a real sword, master.
jokes.setup: Gob expecting trick sword
jokes.punchline: Buster reveals it's actually deadly
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - turns trick into actual mortal danger

jokes.text: It's a real sword, master. Oh-ho!
jokes.setup: Audience expects fake sword for magic trick
jokes.punchline: Buster reveals it's actually real, audience gets excited
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building tension for upcoming disaster.

jokes.text: My brother's widow. It's- [Bleeps] up.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder introduces woman named Candida
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions inappropriate family relationship
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction delivered with casual indifference.

jokes.text: The 'How'd he do dats?' ate it up. Sorry. That's what we call the audience.
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder using insider magic terminology
jokes.punchline: Condescending term for audience members delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Tony Wonder's professional arrogance.

jokes.text: Oh, it still needs three stamps.
jokes.setup: Gob reveals the Subway card
jokes.punchline: Buster notes its incomplete status with childlike observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster - focuses on practical details while missing the bigger picture

jokes.text: He was doing really well. Had his first hop-on.
jokes.setup: Michael worried about George Michael driving
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reports first 'hop-on' like it's a driving milestone
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback making stair car terminology feel like legitimate driving concept.

jokes.text: She's still trying to get the stairs unwedged from the emergency room overhang.
jokes.setup: Learning about the hop-on incident
jokes.punchline: Reveals the accident happened at the hospital
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the driving accident happened at the medical facility

jokes.text: But once I got in there, the appendix wasn't so inflamed. D'oh.
jokes.setup: Doctor reporting on Michael's surgery
jokes.punchline: Casually admits unnecessary surgery with Homer Simpson exclamation
jokes.explanation: Dark medical malpractice comedy with pop culture reference softening the blow.

jokes.text: I want to show these interns what the best doctor in Southern California can do.
jokes.setup: Dr. Stein wants to operate again for infection
jokes.punchline: Upgrades his own reputation from county to state to region
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of doctor's ego inflation throughout episode.

jokes.text: The F.B.I. Has matched the design of the attic to one of Saddam Hussein's Iraqi houses.
jokes.setup: FBI tracking George Sr. from Tobias's video
jokes.punchline: Attic design somehow matches Saddam's house architecture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation connecting family dysfunction to international terrorism.

jokes.text: Booyah!
jokes.setup: FBI video shows George Sr. making demands
jokes.punchline: Tobias appears in background with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Tobias inadvertently appears in incriminating evidence

jokes.text: [Narrator] Actually, he'd been hiding inside the dumbwaiter for over 20 minutes waiting for someone to use the word 'wonder.'
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder's dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the absurd preparation time for one word cue
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator deflation of dramatic moment showing ridiculous commitment to entrance timing.

jokes.text: I grant him magic sanctuary. That's fine. I second the sanctuary. But with a clause of silence.
jokes.setup: Gob wants Buster included in business discussion
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake magic law terminology to exclude Buster
jokes.explanation: Great fake formality comedy with made-up magical legal terms.

jokes.text: Look at Tiagra, my girl. Don't look at her. 'Cause you look at her and it affects her in a way that I don't feel comfortable with. Or go for it. I don't care, whatever.
jokes.setup: Tony describing his assistant
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instructions about looking at her revealing insecurity
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's insecurity and controlling nature through contradictory statements

jokes.text: See, we got the abscess, but I think we might have left some snippers in there. It's not very likely, but one of the assistants having taken them is even less likely.
jokes.setup: Doctor reporting on second surgery success
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions possible surgical instrument left inside patient
jokes.explanation: Escalating medical malpractice comedy with absurd alternative explanation.

jokes.text: Well, uh, we had a little whoopsie. See, we got the abscess, but I think we might have left some snippers in there.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about his surgery
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually admits to leaving surgical instruments inside patient
jokes.explanation: Dark medical humor with childlike euphemism 'whoopsie' for malpractice

jokes.text: We leased the top floor to another company and moved down here. But we can stay in business without having to build the extra houses. We did it!
jokes.setup: Michael thinks company has failed without his control
jokes.punchline: Employees solved problem and are celebrating success
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal showing Michael's control obsession was the problem.

jokes.text: He cut off my- [Bleeps] fingers! I'm not kidding! He cut off my- [Bleeps] fingers!
jokes.setup: Magic trick gone wrong with real sword
jokes.punchline: Gob screaming about actual injury while audience thinks it's part of act
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy with audience misunderstanding real injury as performance.

jokes.text: Handle first. Handle first. [Screams] Oh, my fingers!
jokes.setup: Buster trying to hand Gob the sword properly
jokes.punchline: Accidentally cuts off Gob's fingers despite trying to be careful
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - trying to be safe makes the accident worse

jokes.text: I've shortened your calf muscles to theoretically increase your jumping ability. But if it works, I could be the best in the world.
jokes.setup: Doctor treating Michael's leg injury
jokes.punchline: Admits to experimental surgery for personal glory
jokes.explanation: Peak medical malpractice comedy with absurd body modification for personal achievement.

jokes.text: My middle finger and index finger are- Switched for the first time ever. Nice job, Frank. Amazing.
jokes.setup: Doctor reattached Gob's severed fingers
jokes.punchline: Fingers are switched but doctor is praised for groundbreaking achievement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy treating medical error as revolutionary breakthrough.

jokes.text: You're not half the man he was! This kid's got moves.
jokes.setup: FBI thinks video shows terrorist training
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s family argument mistaken for terrorist propaganda
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist callback transforming family dysfunction into terrorism evidence.
